content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are playing a game with an array AAA of length 2⋅N2\cdot N2⋅N. The game consists of NNN rounds. In each round:


Alice chooses **any** element from the current array, adds its value to her score, and removes it from the array.
Bob then removes the **leftmost** element of the remaining array (without affecting any score).
This continues until the array is empty. Determine the **maximum** score that Alice can achieve by playing optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the total number of rounds in the game.
The next line contains 2⋅N2\cdot N2⋅N space-separated integers — the elements of the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **maximum** score that Alice can achieve by playing optimally.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

Initial array: A=[5,4,3,2]A = [5, 4, 3, 2]A=[5,4,3,2]

Bob removes 444, A=[3,2]A = [3, 2]A=[3,2]
Alice picks 333, Score = 888, A=[2]A = [2]A=[2]
Bob removes 222, A=[]A = []A=[]
Final score: 888

Initial array: A=[3,5]A = [3, 5]A=[3,5]

Bob removes 333, A=[]A = []A=[]
Final score: 555

Test Case 3:

Alice picks 666, Score = 666, A=[1,2,3,4,5]A = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]A=[1,2,3,4,5]
Bob removes 111, A=[2,3,4,5]A = [2, 3, 4, 5]A=[2,3,4,5]
Alice picks 555, Score = 111111, A=[2,3,4]A = [2, 3, 4]A=[2,3,4]
Bob removes 222, A=[3,4]A = [3, 4]A=[3,4]
Alice picks 444, Score = 151515, A=[3]A = [3]A=[3]
Bob removes 333, A=[]A = []A=[]
Final score: 151515
